completion_promises.criterion_text: gate_check(...) orchestrates the full read → evaluate → write → publish/transition sequence
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented gate_check() in src/forge/gating/wrappers.py performing: read_priors → read_adjustments(approved_only=True) → read_rules → evaluate_gate (pure) → record_decision (SQLite) → write_to_graphiti → mode-dispatch (AUTO_APPROVE/HARD_STOP/FLAG_FOR_REVIEW/MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL). Verified by TestGateCheckOrchestratesFullSequence::test_auto_approve_persists_decision_and_returns_outcome.

completion_promises.criterion_text: F9: read_adjustments(approved_only=True) is the only filter point for unapproved adjustments
completion_promises.evidence: gate_check calls deps.adjustments_reader.read_adjustments(approved_only=True) unconditionally — there is no other code path that consults adjustments. Verified by TestApprovedOnlyIsAlwaysTrue::test_unapproved_adjustments_never_reach_evaluate_gate which asserts every recorded call carries approved_only=True.

completion_promises.criterion_text: F10/Group E: gate decision recorded durably even if notification publish fails — SQLite write completes BEFORE publish; publish failure surfaces as operational signal but does NOT roll back
completion_promises.evidence: record_decision() and record_paused_build() are both awaited before publish_request(); publish failure propagates as ApprovalPublishError but no rollback is performed. Verified by TestGateDecisionDurableEvenIfPublishFails::test_publish_failure_does_not_roll_back_sqlite which queues a publish exception and asserts repo.decisions and repo.paused are still populated.

completion_promises.criterion_text: F5/Group E: pausing and publishing are observed as a single consistent transition — status query NEVER reports PAUSED without a corresponding bus publish having been issued
completion_promises.evidence: _atomic_pause_and_publish() enforces ordering: record_paused_build → transition_to_paused → publish_request with no awaits between the SQLite/state-machine writes and the publish. Verified by TestPauseAndPublishAtomicity::test_record_paused_row_precedes_state_paused_precedes_publish using order_log assertions.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A: Approval from Rich resumes the paused build — approve response → state machine resumes from the gated stage
completion_promises.evidence: _dispatch_response routes decision='approve' to mark_resumed + transition_to_running, returning GateOutcome.RESUMED. Verified by TestApprovalDecisionsDriveStateMachine::test_approve_resumes_build.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A: Rejection from Rich ends the paused build — reject response → CANCELLED transition with Rich's reason recorded
completion_promises.evidence: _dispatch_response routes decision='reject' to mark_cancelled + transition_to_cancelled with reason=response.notes (or REASON_REJECT default). Verified by TestApprovalDecisionsDriveStateMachine::test_reject_cancels_with_reason and test_reject_without_notes_uses_default_reason.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A: Override from Rich skips the stage and the build continues — override response → stage marked overridden, build proceeds
completion_promises.evidence: _dispatch_response routes decision='override' to mark_overridden(stage_label, reason) + transition_to_running, returning GateOutcome.OVERRIDDEN. Verified by TestApprovalDecisionsDriveStateMachine::test_override_marks_overridden_and_continues.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D @regression: re-emits its approval request on restart — boot-time hook reads paused_builds and re-publishes; the persisted request_id is used (not re-derived)
completion_promises.evidence: recover_paused_builds() iterates list_paused_builds() and re-publishes via _build_request_envelope(request_id=snap.request_id) — the persisted value is passed verbatim, never re-derived. Verified by TestRecoverPausedBuildsReusesPersistedRequestId::test_reemits_one_envelope_per_paused_build_with_persisted_id which uses a deliberately weird persisted request_id and asserts the published envelope carries it.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D @edge-case: Cancelling from CLI behaves as rejection — forge cancel FEAT-XXX invokes TASK-CGCP-008's inject_cli_cancel, build → CANCELLED with reason='cli cancel'
completion_promises.evidence: cli_cancel_build() looks up the persisted PausedBuildSnapshot and delegates to deps.injector.inject_cli_cancel(build_id, stage_label, attempt_count, correlation_id) using the SQLite-persisted attempt_count verbatim (per TASK-CGCP-008 idempotency contract). The synthetic injector publishes onto the standard mirror subject so the subscriber's dedup gate arbitrates. Verified by TestCliBridgesDelegateToSyntheticInjector::test_cli_cancel_invokes_inject_cli_cancel_with_persisted_count.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D @edge-case: Skipping from CLI overrides current stage only — forge skip FEAT-XXX invokes TASK-CGCP-008's inject_cli_skip, current stage overridden, build continues
completion_promises.evidence: cli_skip_stage() mirrors cli_cancel_build but delegates to inject_cli_skip — the synthetic 'override' response flows through the same subscriber path so _dispatch_response's override branch fires (mark_overridden + transition_to_running). Verified by TestCliBridgesDelegateToSyntheticInjector::test_cli_skip_invokes_inject_cli_skip.

completion_promises.criterion_text: F4/Group D Scenario Outline: typed-vs-dict rehydration — every read of the response goes through resume_value_as(ApprovalResponsePayload, raw)
completion_promises.evidence: Both call sites that consume an await_response result (gate_check main path and _dispatch_response defer path) wrap the raw value via resume_value_as(ApprovalResponsePayload, raw). Verified by TestResumeValueRehydration::test_dict_response_is_rehydrated_into_typed_payload which feeds a plain-dict response through the wrapper and asserts the state machine reaches the correct typed-decision branch — direct attribute access on a dict would AttributeError.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D @edge-case: Per-build response routing — two builds paused on different stages — a response addressed to one does not affect the other
completion_promises.evidence: Each gate_check call binds its own build_id when calling subscriber.await_response(build_id, ...) — the subscriber's per-build queue (TASK-CGCP-007) routes responses by build_id. Verified by TestPerBuildResponseRouting::test_two_concurrent_paused_builds_resolve_independently which runs two gate_check calls under asyncio.gather and asserts each terminates with its own response (build A approved → RESUMED; build B rejected → CANCELLED).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group D @edge-case: Max-wait ceiling reached — when total wait reaches ApprovalConfig.max_wait_seconds, the pause ends per the configured fallback; reason recorded against the gate
completion_promises.evidence: When subscriber.await_response returns None (max-wait reached, per TASK-CGCP-007 contract), gate_check transitions the build to CANCELLED via REASON_MAX_WAIT and returns GateOutcome.TIMED_OUT. ASSUM-003 ceiling-fallback policy is currently CANCELLED — flagged in the concerns array for the forge-pipeline-config feature to revisit. Verified by TestMaxWaitCeiling::test_subscriber_returning_none_triggers_cancelled_fallback.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Both files follow the project code-style guide: PEP 8, black-compatible 88-col formatting, full type hints, snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants, f-string formatting, context-manager-style async patterns. No bare except, no silent error swallowing — every catch logs and either re-raises or surfaces an operational signal. Imports are ordered (stdlib → third-party → first-party). pytest run is clean (24 passed, 0 failed); adjacent test directories run clean (302 passed) confirming no regressions.
